steps.title: Find the key signal in the claim
steps.content: The researchers claim caterpillar sounds are “directed primarily at predators,” not at members of the same species. To support this claim, we need evidence showing the sounds function as a threat response—something triggered by predator attacks, not ongoing communication between caterpillars.

steps.title: Match the answer to the signal
steps.content: Choice A directly demonstrates this: sounds occur exclusively during simulated attacks and stop within ten seconds after, which is the behavioral signature of a defensive mechanism. The other choices address the diversity and specificity of sounds produced, but none establish whether the sounds function as predator defense or intraspecific communication. That matches choice A — A is correct.
